nodes.label: AI reliability for conceptual vs mathematical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would mostly stick with AI responses to my research questions in the instance that there is no math involved in giving an accurate response

nodes.label: AI consistency across rephrased questions
nodes.grounding_span: AI tends to pander to their sensibilities and changes its answer depending on how they phrase a question

nodes.label: AI source accuracy and verifiability
nodes.grounding_span: the sources were either retracted from the journals they were published in or entirely hallucinated

nodes.label: AI as preliminary research guide
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for final decisions
nodes.grounding_span: I used AI mostly when researching the relevant technical details for electrical components

nodes.label: AI ability to vet empirical formulas
nodes.label_negative: AI inability to vet empirical formulas
nodes.grounding_span: getting the AI to be able to vet the efficacy and specific use cases of empirical formula/equations

nodes.label: Research efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: its alot more work to double-check the AI than it is to find a section in a familiar reference text

nodes.label: Accuracy and trustworthiness of information
nodes.grounding_span: the fundamental premise behind a good research tool is that it provides accurate and unbiased information

nodes.label: Safety and ethical responsibility
nodes.grounding_span: if the information is going to have ramifications that effect people directly in terms of health and safety

nodes.label: Preference for AI in conceptual research
nodes.grounding_span: I would mostly stick with AI responses to my research questions in the instance that there is no math involved

nodes.label: Skepticism due to inconsistency
nodes.grounding_span: The inconsistency tends to make me skeptical of the AI response

nodes.label: Frustration with hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: I have experienced plenty of hallucinations regrading the reference material

nodes.label: Desire for AI to vet empirical formulas
nodes.grounding_span: Another area that would be good to focus on is getting the AI to be able to vet the efficacy and specific use cases of empirical formula/equations

nodes.label: Consistency-checking verification strategy
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to ask more questions that are similar to the original question but with more complexity that includes parts of the original response

nodes.label: Safety-tiered verification approach
nodes.grounding_span: if the information is going to have ramifications that effect people directly in terms of health and safety, I use an alternative and more rigid verification process
